      LEAVE DURING PERIOD OF EXERCISE.

   IN THE MENTIONED EXERCISE OF NAVAL CONTROL OF

   A. IN TRANSIT, ENTERING AREA OF SAR EXERCISE.

   C. MOORED AT A BRAZILIAN PORT AND ESTIMATED TO
      LEAVE DURING PERIOD OF EXERCISE.

   BE TRANSMITTED, BY E-MAIL TO COMCONTRAM WITH COPY TO
   COMANDO DO DISTRITO NAVAL OF MARITIME AREA WHERE THE

   WHERE THE INTERESTED ONE CAN OBTAIN IT THROUGH LINK
   EXERCICIOS DE CNTM/TRANSOCEANIC/FORMAT ALFA IN PORTUGUESE
   AND ENGLISH LANGUAGES. THIS FORM MAY ALSO BE PROVIDED
   BY REPRESENTATIVES OF MILITARY ORGANIZATIONS INVOLVED IN
   THE EXERCISE TO THE MASTERS OF MERCHANT SHIPS AT BERTH.

WESTERN MEDITERRANEAN SEA.
1. EXERCISE NOBLE MARINER 12 IS A MULTINATIONAL NATO EXERCISE
   24 SEP THRU 07 OCT IN AREA BETWEEN
   40-00N NORTHWARD AND 003-00E 011-00E.
2. A NUMBER OF AIR, SURFACE AND SUB-SURFACE UNITS FROM
   VARIOUS NATIONS ARE PARTICIPATING.
3. NAVAL COOPERATION AND GUIDANCE TO SHIPPING (NCAGS)
   ORGANIZATION WILL BE ESTABLISHED TO INTERACT WITH MERCHANT
   SHIPPING AND SUPPORT NAVAL OPERATIONS. NAVAL VESSELS AND
   AIRCRAFT REGULARLY EXERCISE PREVENTATIVE MEASURES IN
   ORDER TO SECURE THE CONTINUOUS AND INNOCENT PASSAGE OF
   MERCHANT SHIPPING DURING LIVE OPERATIONS INCLUDING COUNTER
   PIRACY. FOR THIS REASON, ALL MERCHANT VESSELS WITHIN OR
   ON PASSAGE THROUGH THESE AREAS ARE INVITED TO PARTICIPATE
   IN THE EXERCISE ON A NO-COST/NO-DELAY BASIS.
4. FOR VOLUNTARY PARTICIPATION BY MERCHANT VESSELS,
   EAST BOUND VESSELS ARE REQUESTED TO REPORT 12 HOURS
   BEFORE PASSING 003-00E. WEST BOUND VESSELS 12 HOURS
   BEFORE PASSING 011-00E AND VESSELS LEAVING PORTS
   WITHIN THE EXERCISE AREA SHOULD REPORT SIX HOURS
   BEFORE SAILING TO THE NATO SHIPPING CENTRE:
   E-MAIL: INFO@SHIPPING.NATO.INT,
   PHONE: 44 19 2395 6574, FAX: 44 192 3956 5754.
5. THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION WILL BE REQUIRED:
   A. VESSEL NAME.
   B. IMO NUMBER.
   C. INMARSAT AND E-MAIL CONTACT DETAILS.
   D. CURRENT POSITION.
   E. TRANSIT SPEED THROUGH AREA.
   F. INTENDED MOVEMENTS.
6. ALL MERCHANT VESSELS WITHIN OR APPROACHING THE EXERCISE
   AREA MAY BE CONTACTED BY PARTICIPATING NAVAL VESSELS.
7. CANCEL THIS MSG 08 OCT 12.

6. MERCHANT SHIPS REQUESTING ENTRANCE TO THE SHIPPING RISK AREA (SRA)

   A. UNO CHARLIE PAPA ROMEO,

7. TRAFFIC SHOULD MAINTAIN CONTINUOUS CONTACT WITH BRAZILIAN
   NAVY SHIPS IN PRC INSTRUCTIONS FOR PREPARATION OF THE
   TEXT MESSAGE FORMAT ALPHA. THE DATA BEING EMPLOYED
   DWEVE EXPRESS GROUP BY DATE-TIME (GDH), WHICH MUST
   BE EXPRESSED IN DAYLIGHT TIME ZULU IN THE
   FOLLOWING FORMAT: DDHHHHZMMMAA.
8. FORMAT ALFA:
   A. DATA OF SHIP:
      (1) NAME OF SHIP.
      (2) INDICATIVE INTERNATIONAL RADIO.
      (3) TYPE OF SHIP.
      (4) FLAG OF REGISTRATION.
      (5) IMO NUMBER.
      (6) PORT OF REGISTRATION.
      (7) LENGTH.
      (8) BREADTH.
      (9) MAXIMUM DRAUGHT FOR PRESENT VOYAGE.
      (10) GROSS TONNAGE.
      (11) SPEED:
          (A) SPEED OF CRUISE.
          (B) MAXIMUM SPEED.
          (C) MINIMUM SPEED.
      (12) MAIN CHARACTERISTIC OF VESSEL FOR VISUAL RECOGNITION.
      (13) MARINE PORTABLE SERVICE NUMBER MMSI-IDENTIFICATION.
      (14) NAME OF COMMUNICATION STATION ON WATCH.
      (15) NUMBER AND INMARSAT PHONE.
      (16) INMARSAT.
      (17) NUMBER OF TELEX INMARSAT.
      (18) NUMBER OF DATA TRANSMISSION.
      (19) OTHER MEANS OF COMMUNICATION, INCLUDING E-MAIL.
   B. DATA OF VOYAGE:
      (1) PURPOSE OF MOVEMENT, DESCRIPTION OF VOYAGE.
      (2) LAST VISITED PORT/COUNTRY/ETD.
      (3) NEXT DESTINATION PORT AND ETA.
      (4) PRESENT POSITION.
      (5) DTG AND POSITION OF ENTRANCE IN THE AREA AND
          OTHER COORDINATES OF THE ROUTE POINTS,
          INSIDE THE AREA, ACCORDING TO THE VOYAGE PLAN
          (DTG-LATITUDE/LONGITUDE).
      (6) POSITION AND DTG OF DEPARTURE FROM THE AREA.
   C. DATA OF VESSEL OPERATOR:
     (1) NAME OF THE OWNER/RENTER.
     (2) E-MAIL OF THE OWNER/RENTER.
     (3) PHONE NUMBER OF THE OWNER/RENTER.
     (4) FAX NUMBERS OF THE OWNER/RENTER.
   D. CARGO DATA:
     (1) QUANTITY AND NATURE OF THE MAIN CARGO.
     (2) NAME AND ADDRESS OF THE MAIN CARGO.
     (3) ORIGN OF THE MAIN CARGO.
     (4) DESTINY OF THE MAIN CARGO.
     (5) FINAL DESTINY OF THE MAIN CARGO.
     (6) SPECIAL CONDITIONS APPLICABLE TO THE
         PRESENT ATUAL OPERATION.
